articles.title: Biocontrol facility takes aim at invasive insect threatening forest
articles.description: The hemlock woolly adelgid — named because it is covered in white woolly material — can cause foliage loss and even kill eastern hemlock trees. A team at Acadia University are rearing specialized predatory insects they're hoping can control these pests. The CBC's Gareth Hampshire suited up to get into the facility.

articles.title: Eastern Visayas swelters under dangerous heat index levels
articles.description: TACLOBAN CITY — Several areas in Eastern Visayas continued to experience dangerous heat conditions on Thursday, prompting authorities to urge residents to stay hydrated and avoid prolonged sun exposure. According to the Department of Science and Technology–Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (DOST-Pagasa), Borongan City in Eastern Samar recorded the highest heat index in the region at 45 degrees Celsius, placing it under the “danger” category. READ: HEAT INDEX WATCH: 49 areas to reach ‘danger’ level on Thursday, May 14 Guiuan, also in Eastern Samar, registered a heat index of 43°C, while Maasin City in Southern Leyte reached 44°C. [...]... Keep on reading: Eastern Visayas swelters under dangerous heat index levels

articles.title: St. Mary's mayor hopes clean up of rancid fish sauce plant can start this summer
articles.description: The Town of St. Mary's and Newfoundland and Labrador government have issued a new tender to remove rotten fish sauce from a dilapidated St. Mary's plant — which St. Mary's Mayor Steve Ryan can end a decades-long saga.

articles.title: Blackchin tilapia reportedly found off Pattaya coast
articles.description: Fisheries officials are rushing to inspect seawater in Pattaya on Thursday after freshwater blackchin tilapia were reportedly caught off the beach in the resort city in Chon Buri.

articles.title: Urban Art Biennale: Rust, dust and decay revamps Germany's Völklingen ironworks
articles.description: At one of Europe’s most imposing industrial relics, urban artists are transforming rust, dust and decay into a giant open-air gallery. The Urban Art Biennale has returned to Germany’s Völklingen Ironworks, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.title: Scientists develop an autonomous robot to map endangered coral reefs
articles.description: Researchers in the United States have developed an autonomous underwater robot that can listen to coral reefs, track marine life and identify biodiversity hotspots.

articles.title: Invalides monument in Paris: 350 years of care for wounded soldiers and war victims
articles.description: World-famous as the resting place of Napoleon, the gilded dome of the Invalides in Paris has drawn millions of visitors, but behind the landmark’s grand façade was a lesser-known mission: serving as a home and hospital for wounded soldiers and victims of war for more than 350 years.

articles.title: Bird-snagging netting is 'an expensive death trap'

articles.title: Horoscope for Thursday, May 14, 2026
articles.description:
Moon Alert

Avoid shopping (except food and gas) and important decisions from 4 p.m. to 10 p.m. After that, the moon moves from Aries into Taurus.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

A positive day

This is a powerful week for you, and yet again, you have another powerful day! This is why your ability to get things done, especially by working with others or in conjunction with groups, is superb. Go after what you want. Doors will open for you. Make the most of this!

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

An average day

Today the energy is mixed because, in one way, you want to get out there, fly your colors, and talk to everyone. Yet, in another way, you want to pull in and hide or work alone or behind the scenes. Juggle these opposing energies as best you can. Financial decisions are favored.

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

A positive day

This is a friendly, upbeat day! In particular, it's a day that favors romance, love affairs, and socializing with others. Today and this evening are great times for a date. (Please note: Your partner might be high energy, even aggressive.)

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

An average day

This continues to be a high-visibility time for you because, yet again, you are noticed more than usual by others. Obviously, you can use this to your advantage if you want to propose something or lead a project. Your strong ambition will give you confidence; plus, this is a popular time for you as well.

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

A positive day

With the sun and Mercury at the top of your chart, you make a great impression on others. Your communication skills are also strong. Today you want to meet new faces, see new places and learn new things. Meanwhile, a platonic friendship might turn romantic.

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

An average day

Travel and learn as much as possible today. Publishing is favored. This is also an excellent day to settle disputes about inheritances or shared property because people will be cooperative. Meanwhile, romance and affection will flourish today. Reach out to someone special.

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

A positive day

Be patient with partners, spouses, and close friends at this time because fiery Mars is opposite your sign, which can make you annoyed with others. Fortunately, this is a great time to travel for pleasure. It's also a good time for financial discussions. Romance is in the air!

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

A positive day

Roll up your sleeves because you're ready to work! (Admittedly, you need more sleep now, so don't push yourself too hard.) Romantic relationships will be passionate today and also affectionate. Discussions with someone might lead to commitment.

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

A positive day

This is a work hard/party hard day! Expect to accomplish a lot. Relations with partners, spouses and close friends are also blessed. Meanwhile, sports, athletic competitions and romance will sizzle! Have fun!

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

A positive day

This is a pleasant time for you because you have opportunities to play and socialize — or relax. Many of you will enjoy sports events and fun activities with kids. Meanwhile, a work-related romance is likely. Cozy attachments with others are blessed today.

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

A positive day

Home and family are your primary focus, and fun activities with kids will appeal. Note that this is also a romantic day and a great time for a date or making the first move if there's someone you admire. Why not? Life is short.

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

An average day

You might entertain at home today. Certainly, working in conjunction with others will help you accomplish the most. This is a great day for short trips, sales, marketing, teaching, acting and writing. You'll also enjoy learning. Romance is blessed.

If today is your birthday

American filmmaker and philanthropist George Lucas (1944) shares your birthday today. You're compassionate, determined and enthusiastic. You have great powers of perception and imagination. This is a slower-paced year. Take time to rejuvenate your energy and outlook on the world. Focus on business and personal relationships, especially with people who are supportive, kind and have your back.

HEAT INDEX WATCH: Six Calabarzon areas face dangerous levels
articles.description: LUCENA CITY, Quezon – At least six areas in the Calabarzon (Cavite, Laguna, Batangas, Rizal, Quezon) region are expected to experience dangerous heat index levels on Thursday and Friday, May 14 and 15, amid the continuing extreme heat. In its 5 p.m. forecast issued Wednesday, the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) said Sangley Point in Cavite City, Cavite is forecast to register a heat index of 44 degrees Celsius on Thursday and 45°C on Friday. Cavite City has been experiencing dangerous heat conditions in recent days, with the heat index reaching as high as 45°C on April [...]... Keep on reading: HEAT INDEX WATCH: Six Calabarzon areas face dangerous levels

articles.title: WATCH: This Deer Got So Drunk on Fermented Plants That Police Had to Warn Drivers to Be Careful Nearby
articles.description: Somewhere in rural eastern France, a deer recently discovered the joys of fermentation. It’s good for your gut. It provides a wide range of beneficial probiotics that improve digestion. It can also get you f—ked up. Police in the Saône-et-Loire region posted video footage of the deer sprinting through a field, leaping about—frolicking, really—after having [...] The post WATCH: This Deer Got So Drunk on Fermented Plants That Police Had to Warn Drivers to Be Careful Nearby appeared first on VICE .
